title: "abc -liberty ..." maps to LUTs not the provided library if the "-dress" option is used.
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.25+60 (git sha1 611f71c67, clang 14.0.6-2 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

With this file `add4.v`:
```
module top(input [3:0] a, input [3:0] b, output [3:0] q);
    assign q = a + b;
endmodule
```
and running this script:
```
read_verilog add4.v
proc
hierarchy -top top

synth -flatten

opt_clean -purge

abc -liberty  sky130_fd_sc_hd__ff_100C_1v95.lib -dress

opt_clean -purge

write_verilog -noexpr out.v
```

the output file `out.v` contains all `$lut` cells such as:
```
\$lut  #(
    .LUT(4'h7),
    .WIDTH(32'd2)
  ) _08_ (
    .A({ a[0], b[0] }),
    .Y(_01_)
  );
```

## To reproduce
[dress.tar.gz](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/10493332/dress.tar.gz)

```
gunzip dress.tar.gz
tar xvf dress.tar
cd dress
yosys add4-dress.ys
```







### Expected Behavior

I would expect `out.v` to contain a mapping to the cells in the sky130 Liberty file.   That
 is the behavior if the `-dress` option to the `abc` command is removed:

```
  sky130_fd_sc_hd__xnor2_1 _17_ (
    .A(b[3]),
    .B(a[3]),
    .Y(_07_)
  );
```


### Actual Behavior

the output file `out.v` contains all `$lut` cells such as:
```
\$lut  #(
    .LUT(4'h7),
    .WIDTH(32'd2)
  ) _08_ (
    .A({ a[0], b[0] }),
    .Y(_01_)
  );
```
